title: Modules & Scope 📦
description: Organizing your code into manageable pieces.

slides.title: The Infinite File 📜
slides.content:
So far, we've written everything inside `main.rs`. For a real app, this file would become 100,000 lines long. Nightmare.

Rust uses a strict **Module System** to split code across multiple files and folders.

slides.title: Defining a Module 📦

quizData.question: What specifically is the purpose of a Rust Module?

options.text: To logically group related functions and manage scope cleanly within your project.

quizData.explanation: Modules prevent massive monolithic files entirely by cleanly categorizing behavior locally.

slides.title: Splitting Files 📁

quizData.question: If you rigorously declare `mod physics;` completely globally inside `main.rs`, the compiler smoothly expects to explicitly find a dedicated file named `physics.rs` seamlessly sitting directly next to it.
quizData.explanation: This intelligently exactly binds the file structurally to the massive active root tree cleanly successfully.
